meaning: adj.去势的,柔弱的,被阉割的
examples: No man here is emasculated by his high-achieving wife.
I really dislike the fact that Asian males are constantly emasculated, whether it's American TV or films.
A referendum to authorise renegotiations would have been risky too (Mr Cameron would have been seriously emasculated if the negotiations failed).
Men do not need to be emasculated every time a woman takes a step forward.
Even Britain, usually a friend of market-opening, does not want to see the Royal Mail emasculated.
